benchmark_name: MILU: A Multi-task Indic Language Understanding Benchmark
humanities_role_explanation: Uses humanities content (Arts & Humanities, Social Sciences, History, Literature, etc.) both as evaluation data and as a core domain for assessing multilingual LLM capabilities in culturally relevant areas
benchmark_modalities_explanation: Text-only multiple-choice questions covering 41 subjects across 8 domains in 11 Indic languages
analytical_tasks_explanation: Evaluates factual knowledge and reasoning across culturally relevant domains like Arts & Humanities (26.1%), Social Sciences, History, Literature, and Law & Governance, requiring both linguistic competence and cultural understanding

general_purpose_benchmark.note: Multi-domain benchmark covering 8 domains and 41 subjects across multiple languages

evaluates_generative_llm.note: GPT-4o, GPT-4o-mini, Gemini-1.5-Pro, Gemini-1.5-Flash, Llama-3.1/3.2 series, Gemma-2 series, and various Indic language models

llm_is_primary_system.note: LLMs are the primary systems being benchmarked for multilingual and cultural understanding

arts_humanities_data.note: Language and Linguistics (ASJC 1206), plus substantial Arts & Humanities content including local history, arts, festivals, literature, and cultural knowledge

quantitative_metrics.note: Accuracy scores reported across languages, domains, and shot settings

english_language.note: Paper written in English, benchmark includes English alongside 10 Indic languages

summary: MILU introduces a comprehensive multilingual benchmark for evaluating LLMs across 11 Indic languages, spanning 8 domains and 41 subjects with emphasis on culturally relevant knowledge. The benchmark reveals significant performance gaps in culturally specific domains like Arts & Humanities compared to general STEM fields.
key_findings: GPT-4o achieved highest performance at 74% average accuracy. Models perform significantly better in high-resource languages and general domains like STEM, but struggle with culturally relevant areas like Arts & Humanities and Social Sciences. Open multilingual models outperformed language-specific fine-tuned models.
paper_name: Fine-tuning Large Language Models for Automated Pun Translation
